name: Quagga

visual_attributes.morphology: Equid with zebra-like front and horse-like rear
distinctive_features: Brown and white stripes primarily on the front part of the body
visual_attributes.size_and_scale: 257 cm (8 ft 5 in) long, 125–135 cm (4 ft 1 in – 4 ft 5 in) tall at the shoulders
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: Stripes boldest on head and neck, fading towards the rear

domain_specific_visual.striping_pattern: Limited to front, fading towards rear
domain_specific_visual.body_proportion: Mares longer and slightly taller than stallions

secondary_functions: Part of the ecosystem as prey

domain_specific_functional.ecological_role: Competed with domesticated animals for forage

relationships.description: The quagga is a subspecies of the plains zebra.
Shared similar striping patterns with the extinct southernmost population of Burchell's zebra.

contextual_attributes.geographic_range: Karoo of Cape Province and southern Orange Free State, South Africa
contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: Symbol of extinction and conservation efforts

contrastive_constraints: NOT fully striped like a typical zebra
